{{Quote|The Hardan plague that resulted in the deaths of 98% of the inhabitants of Dagelin Minor was traced to a single shipment of brown nafen that were imported in an attempt to control an infestation of dhuru flies.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Dagelin Minor''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] celestial body located in the [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector]]. A shipment of the flying [[Rodent/Legends|rodents]] known as [[Bogan's brown nafen]] was at one point imported there for the purposes of combating an infestation of [[Dhuru fly|dhuru flies]]. The [[nafen]], however, caused an outbreak of the [[Hardan plague]] that [[Death/Legends|wiped out]] ninety-eight percent of the local population. In response, [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] visited Dagelin Minor on a mercy mission.

==Description==
Dagelin Minor was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="TEA" /> located in the [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor system]], a part of the [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated on the [[Cadma Conduit]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which connected it to the [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc system]] as well as<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector's]]<ref name="EAOC Junkfort">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Junkfort system]]</ref> [[Junkfort system]] and the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] in the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:Bogansbrownnafen.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The introduction of Bogan's brown nafen on Dagelin Minor caused the deaths of 98 percent of the celestial body's population.]]
At some point, Dagelin Minor faced an infestation of [[Dhuru fly|dhuru flies]]. In response, a shipment of [[Bogan's brown nafen]]—[[Diet/Legends|insectivorous]] flying [[Rodent/Legends|rodents]] only found on primarily [[Temperature/Legends|tropical]] [[Planet/Legends|planets]]—was brought onto the celestial body. The [[nafen]], which were notorious for being extremely efficient transmitters of [[Disease/Legends|disease]], caused an outbreak of the [[Hardan plague]] on Dagelin Minor, which led to the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of ninety-eight percent of its population.<ref name="Creatures" />

In response, at some point between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] visited Dagelin Minor on her [[CR90 corvette/Legends|consular starship]], the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], on a well-publicized mercy mission to bring emergency gear and [[Medic/Legends|medical]] supplies to the inhabitants of the celestial body.<ref name="TEA" /> It was eventually discovered that the devastating outbreak had originated from the single shipment of Bogan's brown nafen, and during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], that fact was noted in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the nafen.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dagelin Minor was first mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the celestial body was authored by [[Chuck Truett]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed Dagelin Minor in grid square T-8.<ref name="TEA" />
